title: Male Organs in The Evas
wikitext:
[]: Male Organs in The Evas             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:16 am I know this topic has been addressed before, since I read it while I was going through the pages and pages within this forum. But then the thread sort of went off on a tangent at the end and I didn't think it was ever fully resolved (or as best as fans can resolve anything). And so, I offer up my own theory to why the Evas would have male organs in what we assume to be a female body.. I did a bit of research, and found information that states in 2002 the Federative International Committee on Anatomical Terminology officially renamed a rather obscure gland in the female body to the prostate . And, it's been determined that an embryo is not entirely female in development as was once thought. Rather, it is a blend of male and female components, and only when the second chromosome is determined do the components develop (or degenerate) into their respective organs. After all, female humans have all the same parts as male humans, but in different ways (obviously). So really, male and female is not physically that different, and so it's not ridiculous at all (or simply a mistake on the part of GAINAX) that Evas would have such organs. In fact, in women the prostate actually functions in a very similar way as the male prostate, by producing a fluid similar to the fluid found in semen. And also, I thought I'd mention my view on Eva genitalia. And that is, if we assume the bodies of the Evas are female, with female genitalia and simply lacking the ovaries and the uterus, than none of the pilots would have felt numbness in their genitals. Yes, even Shinji, because the female body has a component analogous to the penis. So, he might've felt really inadequate, but not completely numb. XD Opinions?

[]: Re: Male Organs in The Evas             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jan 27, 2008 11:43 am AsukaChan wrote: In fact, in women the prostate actually functions in a very similar way as the male prostate, by producing a fluid similar to the fluid found in semen.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Jan 27, 2008 11:45 am BrikHaus wrote: There was a discussion about this recently. Does anyone remember where it was?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sun Jan 27, 2008 2:54 pm I did a bit of research, and found information that states in 2002 the Federative International Committee on Anatomical Terminology officially renamed a rather obscure gland in the female body to the prostate.

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Jan 27, 2008 3:54 pm Yes, I realize that. (: I bring this up simply because it seemed like the argument was that it would be utterly impossible for females to have anything like the prostate or the Cowper's glands. I'm not arguing about the intentions of the people who wrote it in, I was trying to suggest that just because these organs are present it doesn't mean that the Evas' bodies cannot be female, since females have the same, or similar organs.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 27, 2008 4:30 pm Okay, we've wanked away the prostate... but how about the Cowper's gland? You can't seriously tell me females have Cowper's glands.

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Jan 27, 2008 7:11 pm Not by the same name, no. Females do have their version though. They have the Bartholin's gland instead of the Cowper's gland, but they have the same function, which is to lubricate.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 27, 2008 7:21 pm AsukaChan wrote: They have the Bartholin's gland instead of the Cowper's gland, but they have the same function, which is to lubricate.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jan 27, 2008 7:42 pm NAveryW wrote: ...Why the deuce would Evangelions need lubrication anyway?

[]:             Quote Post by AsukaChan » Sun Jan 27, 2008 8:18 pm It lubricates, but not in the same way that the Bartholin's does. I should've specified. The Cowper's gland lubricates the urethra in preparation for the sperm. "During sexual arousal each gland produces a clear, viscous secretion known as pre-ejaculate. This fluid helps to lubricate the urethra for spermatozoa to pass through, and to help flush out any residual urine or foreign matter." So basically, they're both glands developed for similar uses, though used in different ways.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jan 27, 2008 8:52 pm Please teach me more, AsukaChan. I know nothing of human biology.
